title: Glandularia longidentata
intro: Glandularia longidentata, also known as Rosita, is a flowering plant species native to South America, belonging to the Verbenaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Glandularia longidentata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Glandularia longidentata is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to promote respiratory health, alleviate cold and flu symptoms, and support immune function.
taxonomy: Glandularia longidentata is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Glandularia longidentata includes its kingdom as Plantae, and its domain as Eukarya. The plant belongs to the clade Angiosperms, which is a group of flowering plants. Within the family Verbenaceae, Glandularia longidentata is part of the genus Glandularia.
